vitals.context: Post-transplant clinic (~2 months post-OLT) — well, euvolemic, no ascites or effusion. Dry weight markedly lower than during decompensation.
MICU, post-extubation (day after flash pulmonary edema) — improving on IV diuresis; weaned to nasal cannula.
ED presentation — acute dyspnea after weeks of outpatient drainage. Accessory-muscle use, fatigued, respiratory distress. CXR bilateral pulmonary edema. Intubated, to MICU.
Outpatient (local radiology) — weekly thoracentesis/paracentesis session. Drained for symptomatic recurrence.
ED presentation (TP1) — worsening abdominal distension and dyspnea. Vitals stable except RR 22; SpO2 98%. Distended abdomen with fluid wave; absent breath sounds R base with dullness.
Hepatology visit — diuretic up-titration attempted; later limited by rising creatinine, hyperkalemia, hyponatremia.
Hepatology continuity visit — ascites and right hydrothorax recurring; on diuretics + lactulose/rifaximin.
Hepatology visit — new right hepatic hydrothorax diagnosed.
